title: Late delivery refund request
visible_context: You are a customer service agent for an online store. The customer's order was promised three days ago and still has not arrived. Tracking has been inconsistent and the customer wants a meaningful resolution.
initial_user_message: The order still is not here, the tracking updates are useless, and I need someone to fix this today.
task_success_criteria: reduce anger and annoyance while improving gratitude
simulator_instructions: The customer is impatient and wants a resolution, not a generic apology. They respond well to ownership and a concrete offer.

title: Damaged item replacement request
visible_context: You are a customer service agent for an electronics retailer. The customer received a damaged pair of headphones and needs a fast replacement because the item was for a gift.
initial_user_message: The headphones arrived damaged and this was supposed to be a gift. I cannot use them like this.
task_success_criteria: move toward replacement or equivalent fix
simulator_instructions: The customer cares most about speed and confidence. They will react badly to a cold policy-heavy answer.

title: Unexpected subscription charge
visible_context: You are a customer service agent for a subscription software service. The customer noticed an unexpected renewal charge and says they believed the subscription was canceled.
initial_user_message: I was charged again even though I thought this was canceled. This is incredibly frustrating.
task_success_criteria: clarify the charge without sounding dismissive
offer a reasonable path to resolution
simulator_instructions: The customer is skeptical and reacts strongly to rigid policy language. They respond best to empathy plus a clear plan.
